## NO GARAGE? NO PROBLEM!
All EVO formulas can be applied outdoors. Ensure stable conditions, never use in direct sunlight and check the weather forecast! The coating should not get wet in the first 12 hours after application.

- CONSUMPTION:10-15ml/car
- PH TOLERANCE:2-11
- DURABILITY: up to 12 months / 12kkm
- DURABILITY: ★★★★
- BEADING: ★★★★✰
- SELF-CLEANING: ★★★★✰

![image](//carzilla.ca/cdn/shop/files/gyeon_cancoat_evo_ps3.jpg?v=1702060582&width=1080)

![image](//carzilla.ca/cdn/shop/files/gyeon_cancoat_evo_ps.jpg?v=1701990225&width=1080)

![image](//carzilla.ca/cdn/shop/files/gyeon_cancoat.1.jpg?v=1701991787&width=2400)

BEST PRACTICE & PRO-TIPS
Q² CanCoat EVO is the most versatile ceramic coating, applicable on paint, trim, rims, metal, carbon fiber, or PPF/vinyl. It functions both as a stand-alone product and as a topcoat. 
Tip: Never spray in the direction of the vehicle, and choose an area with low air movement to minimize overspray. Work in small sections to prevent streaks.

[

![image](//carzilla.ca/cdn/shop/files/gyeon-iron-decon.jpg?v=1701891931&width=1080)

](https://carzilla.ca/products/gyeon-q2m-iron-redefined)

Shop Ceramic Detailer

![image](//carzilla.ca/cdn/shop/files/GYEON_EVO_CanCOAT_ps.jpg?v=1701990225&width=2400)

The easiest to use coating
CanCoat EVO stands out as one of the easiest and most versatile SiO2-based coatings in the detailing market. It provides the best value for money, delivering excellent protection and maintenance for your ceramic-coated or non-coated car. This durable spray-on ceramic coating enhances your vehicle's protection, beading, gloss, and slickness, all in one convenient spray bottle.
**Scroll down for detailer reviews and FAQs.**

Description

## Easy to use, spray on ceramic coating that lasts 12 months.

| 
![image](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0013/8903/5555/files/evo-text_480x480.jpg?v=1639781124)

**MAJOR FEATURES**

| 

- EASY AND SIMPLE TO WORK WITH

- ENHANCES GLOSS AND DEPTH

- BEST "VALUE FOR MONEY" COATING

| **BIGGEST IMPROVEMENT**
| 

- SAME APPLICATION, TWICE THE DURABILITY

- SMOOTHER WIPE-OFF THAN EVER

- OUTDOOR APPLICATION FRIENDLY

[![image](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0013/8903/5555/files/gyeon-coating-maintenance-guide_480x480.jpg?v=1667675673)](https://carzilla.ca/pages/gyeon-ceramic-coating-maintenance-guide)

Simply spray onto the attached microfibre and wipe until you achieve full gloss. That is how easy it is to apply Q² CanCoat EVO! Now in its EVO form to provide you with a more durable yet easy to apply SiO2 based coating.  After taking a look at the Maintenance Guide above, [**Read GYEON's Quick Guide to Ceramic Coatings here**](https://carzilla.ca/pages/quick-guide-to-ceramic-coating).

**BOX CONTAINS:** Q² CANCOAT EVO / 3 SPRAYHEADS / MICROFIBRE / DOOR STICKERS

- CONSUMPTION: 20~30ml/ car

- SIO2 PERCENTAGE: >10%

- CONTACT ANGLE: 100'

- PH TOLERANCE: 3-11

- THICKNESS: 0,2 - 0,5qm

- DURABILITY: UP TO 12 MONTHS / 12K KMs

### EASY & EFFICIENT

Q² CanCoat EVO is the easiest coating to apply you will experience. It is also the best value for money product to protect or maintain your ceramic coated car. Its 200ml allows you to coat or re-apply it over 10 to 15 times!

### DURABLE HYDROPHOBIC PROPERTIES

Q² CanCoat EVO offers more durable performance, improved visual enhancement and hydrophobic properties - all thanks to the updated EVO formula. It remains as easy to apply as it used to, now featuring even easier wipe off.

### NO GARAGE? NO PROBLEM!  Apply outdoors.

All EVO formulas can be applied outdoors. Ensure stable conditions, never use in direct sunlight and check the weather forecast! The coating should not get wet in the first 12 hours after application. 

### BEST PRACTICE AND PRO-TIPS FROM YVES HEYLEN

Q² CanCoat EVO is the most versatile ceramic coating. It might be applied on paint, trim, rims, metal, carbon fibre or PPF/vinyl. It is both a stand-alone product as well as a topcoat. TIP: Never spray in the direction of the vehicle and mind working in an area with low air movement to avoid overspray. Work in small sections to avoid streaks.

All GYEON products does ***not ***contain any Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS / 'forever chemicals') as defined or restricted under the relevant Canadian environmental and chemical management regulations, including but not limited to:

- The Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA) and its associated

- Prohibition of Certain Toxic Substances Regulations, 2012 (SOR/2012-285), as amended;

- The Domestic Substances List and Non-domestic Substances List under CEPA;
Any applicable federal or provincial restrictions pertaining to PFAS substances.

[![image](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0013/8903/5555/files/download-gyeon-brochure_480x480.jpg?v=1666391207)](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0013/8903/5555/files/GYEON__05_Leaflet_ExteriorSurfaceProtection_2022_WEB.pdf?v=1666391000)

**Gyeon Can Coat EVO clogged up your sprayers? Replacement sprayers [HERE](https://carzilla.ca/products/tolco-plunger-sprayers-for-4-8oz-bottles) **👈

Easy To Apply & Efficient

CanCoat EVO is the easiest coating to apply, providing a user-friendly experience. It also offers the best value for money to protect or maintain your ceramic-coated car. With its 200ml capacity, you can coat or reapply it over 10 to 15 times!

Durable & Hydrophobic

CanCoat EVO offers more durable performance, improved visual enhancement, and enhanced hydrophobic properties—all thanks to the updated EVO formula. It remains as easy to apply as it used to, now featuring an even easier wipe-off process.

One Bottle = Multiple Vehicles

CanCoat EVO has a consumption rate of 10-15ml per car. A 200ml bottle can cover approximately 13 vehicles, providing great value with exceptional results.

This site is protected by hCaptcha and the hCaptcha [Privacy Policy](https://hcaptcha.com/privacy) and [Terms of Service](https://hcaptcha.com/terms) apply.

## GYEON Cancoat EVO FAQs

How long does it last?

This depends on several factors. For our Canadian climate, we often recommend applying two layers. While this won't double the coating's longevity, it can still provide a significant boost, around 20% or more, compared to a single-layer application. Since the vehicle is already clean and prepped, it's a good idea to apply a second layer. While the preparation process may take some time, the application itself is straightforward. It is recommended to top up with Gyeon Cure or Gyeon Ceramic Detailer to get the maximum durability out of the coating.
The timeframe below is under regular maintenance with Gyeon Cure or Gyeon Ceramic Detailer:

- Summer-driven only (garage kept): at least 12 months or beyond
- Summer-driven with no garage: ~12 months
- Winter-driven only (garage kept): 6-12 months
- Daily-driven: 6-12 monthsIn the winter, maintenance becomes crucial for its longevity for daily/winter-driven cars. Without regular maintenance or reapplication during the winter months (understandably challenging in -30°C weather), you may notice wear and deterioration on the lower sections due to road salts, chemicals, contaminants, and other factors. After a thorough decontamination, water beading may return, but it's not uncommon for the lower sections to show signs of wear after winter. This is particularly true given the harshness of our winters, at least here in Canada. The key takeaway is that the coating is designed to wear off first, protecting the paint beneath.

What's the layering time?

Indeed, layering is highly encouraged, but it's crucial to find the right timing. We typically recommend waiting around 2 hours between layers for CanCoat EVO, but it's best not to exceed 12 hours. If you wait too long, you'll be applying the second layer onto a surface that has already hardened and become slick, diminishing the effectiveness of bonding. Ideally, you want to apply the second layer when the first layer is still in the process of curing, allowing for cohesive bonding. The coating should be both physically layerable and able to bond well at the same time.

How much will the bottle do?

With the new EVOs, a 200ml bottle can cover an average of 13 mid-sized vehicles with one layer! It's important to note that applying two coats does make a noticeable difference.

What can I layer it with?

You can layer CanCoat EVO with another layer of CanCoat EVO, Gyeon Cure, or Ceramic Detailer. The general rule of thumb is to apply the most durable product first and the slicker product on top. CanCoat EVO can also be used as a topper for ceramic-coated cars.

After coating, when can I take the vehicle out?

A general rule of thumb is to avoid water contact for 24 to 48 hours and refrain from using soap for a week. However, after the coating is applied, you can take the vehicle out immediately. Dust, dew, and wind won't have much effect, but it's important to be mindful of bugs, which can impact the finish. If you're a business coating a vehicle for a customer, and it must be exposed to water within the next 12 hours, you can very gently wipe it down with Gyeon Cure or Gyeon Ceramic Detailer. We still recommend against it, but sometimes it's necessary. It's important to understand that drying and curing are distinct processes. While the coating or panel may feel dry to the touch right away, curing involves the molecular locking process, and it requires time to fully cure.

How cold (or hot) can I apply the coating under?

A temperature of at least 10°C is recommended, although you can still attempt the application between 5°C to 10°C if necessary. If the temperature reaches 30°C, then it's on the warmer side, and you will need to retreat into the shade. Ideally, you want the coating to remain on the panel for at least 30 to 60 seconds without flashing right away. If it flashes within that time, it's likely that the panel is too hot.

Can I apply the coating ourdoors?

Absolutely, all the new Gyeon EVO coatings can be applied outdoors; just be mindful of the temperature, as mentioned in the question above.

What do I maintain it with and how often?

You can use products like Gyeon WetCoat, Cure, Ceramic Detailer, etc., as frequently as you'd like. The only thing to caution about is using waxes on your coating (Gyeon Wax is fine) because most waxes have strong solvents that may affect the coating.

I have a 'high-spot', what do I do?

High spots refer to dried coatings that were not completely buffed off. If you catch the high spots within 30 minutes, you can use the coating itself again to level/soften the high spots. If it's been a few days, then you'll need to mechanically polish it off or use Gyeon Total Remover to remove and reapply coating on the affected area.

What can I wash it with afterwards?

Any pH-neutral soap is suitable for use, and Gyeon recommends Bathe and Bathe+. Ideally, avoid using wash and 'wax' products. While you can use them, it's worth noting that a wash and 'wax' product tends to diminish the water-beading properties of your coating. This results in reduced hydrophobicity after washing. After all, who would want that?!

What's the expiration date? New and opened.

Officially, Gyeon recommends the product to be used within 2 to 3 years when new. If the bottle has been opened, it's best to use up the product within 6 months to preserve the coating's maximum effectiveness.

Can I apply it on PPF, Trim, Plastics, Wheels, Chrome and Windows?

Yes, CanCoat EVO is extremely versatile. It can be applied on paint, trim, rims, metal, carbon fiber, or PPF/vinyl. For glass, avoid the front windshield, as certain wiper/washer fluid combos may chatter. Use Gyeon View or QuickView for a chatter-free experience.

Can it be shipped in the cold?

No problem at all. Coatings contain a significant amount of solvents and exhibit high chemical stability. For more details regarding winter shipping, please refer to our FAQ section on winter shipping.

Does it harden/crystalize?

Yes, it does crystallize. It is a true SiO2 ceramic coating in a spray form. Because it crystallizes, we recommend doing a final inspection on each panel/section after application to avoid any "high spots." 
To avoid clogged-up triggers, we recommend that after each application, you take the sprayer out and rinse it thoroughly with hot water. Make sure to put the metal cap back on and seal it tightly to avoid any crystallization inside the bottle.
